position_summary: Claude's revision is directionally right and preserves the important differentiator: hypothesis generation plus deterministic testing/refusal inside the Claude Science workbench. But repo verification shows the revision has not landed in the artifact of record: the runnable CS narration, scene captions, and capture plan still carry the old overclaims and unresolved money-shot TODOs.

findings.title: Claude's accepted fixes are not implemented in the actual CS demo artifacts
findings.evidence: `docs/demo-video-pack/cs/narration.mjs` still says "one platform that can generate a hypothesis and validate it" and "a connection the literature had never drawn"; `docs/demo-video-pack/SCRIPT_cs-native_2026-07-09.md` still contains the same old narration; `docs/demo-video-pack/cs/scenes.mjs` still has TODOs for opening the b5 `receipt.md` artifact and reaching b6b `review.json`.
findings.concern_path: The debate revision can be correct on paper while the Friday run still records the stale script. That would preserve the exact risks Claude claimed to fix: live/cached ambiguity, novelty inflation, hidden reviewer evidence, and generic UI over receipt proof.
findings.suggested_action: Patch the actual runnable files before recording: `cs/narration.mjs`, `SCRIPT_cs-native_2026-07-09.md`, `cs/scenes.mjs`, and `cs/CAPTURE_PLAN.md`. Treat the debate text as insufficient until the screen-recording harness itself carries the scoped lines and exact receipt shots.

findings.title: The promised live-vs-cached caption is absent, while the scene caption still implies live literature/database calls
findings.evidence: Claude proposed an on-screen caption: `full sweep = CS-native cached-receipt replay · micro-sweep + STAT6 S3 = live`. The repo does not contain that caption. Instead `cs/scenes.mjs` sets b4 to `Claude Science authored the LBD generator - live literature + database calls` and b5 to `22,039 questions in - 30 receipt-backed survivors`, with no cached-replay disclosure. The pipeline README explicitly says the full Stage-1 sweep had `delta 0` live calls and was not a fresh web crawl.
findings.concern_path: A viewer sees live API language immediately followed by 22,039 funnel numbers. Even if the README is honest, the video can still imply the 22k funnel was crawled live in CS.
findings.suggested_action: Put the cached/live boundary on screen during b5, not only in docs: `full 22,039 sweep = cached-receipt replay; live proof = micro-sweep; live falsification = STAT6 S3`. Also change b4 wording to avoid letting `live literature + database calls` bleed into b5.

findings.title: The confident NO is still asserted more strongly than it is captured
findings.evidence: Claude accepted adding a visible NO receipt, but `cs/scenes.mjs` only sets the generic b5 caption `a confident NO on the rest` and still TODOs opening `receipt.md` for the funnel/NAB2. `CAPTURE_PLAN.md` has no solved capture target for a failed-knockdown or SBF2/effect-refuted receipt. The repo does have evidence elsewhere: tracer artifacts show IL2 as gate-fail untested, and `docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md` names SBF2 as effect-refuted, but the CS-native b5 capture path does not visibly bind either to the video.
findings.concern_path: The submission may say `watch it refuse` while showing only funnel counts or NAB2 support. That undercuts the falsification moat and makes the NO feel like narration rather than a demonstrated capability.
findings.suggested_action: Choose one concrete refusal receipt and make it a required frame: IL2 gate-fail untested if using the tracer receipt, SBF2 effect-refuted if using the sweep, or a refuted-for-C example from the stage artifacts. The frame must show the verdict word and the receipt reason, not just the aggregate class count.

findings.title: NAB2 novelty and STAT6 scope remain unpatched in the video text
findings.evidence: `SCRIPT_cs-native_2026-07-09.md` and `cs/narration.mjs` still say NAB2 is `a connection the literature had never drawn` and `Its hardest confounder - refuted live`. The NAB2 finding doc is more nuanced: it reports `6 prior literature co-mentions`, no direct NAB2→Th1/Th2→eczema chain found, and a residual GWAS/LD disease-label caveat while the CRISPRi cis-artifact is what the STAT6 S3 check refutes.
findings.concern_path: A hostile judge can attack both lines as over-broad: `never drawn` conflicts with `ac_lit=6` unless scoped to the mechanistic chain, and `hardest confounder refuted` can sound like all STAT6/GWAS shadow concerns are closed.
findings.suggested_action: Use Claude's revised wording verbatim in the artifact: `six stray co-mentions, but no paper drawing the NAB2-to-Th1/Th2-to-eczema chain`; `the CRISPRi cis-artifact concern was refuted live; the GWAS disease label remains a nomination`.

findings.title: Static overlays are now the right fallback, but the plan still calls the path screen-only/de-risked
findings.evidence: Claude's revision allows prepared artifact views or static overlays for b5/b6b. The repo still says the capture path is de-risked and the `cs/scenes.mjs` header says screen-only gate PASS, while `CAPTURE_PLAN.md` says b5 and b6b require unsolved artifact-thumbnail selectors or scroll mechanics. Those are not equivalent execution modes.
findings.concern_path: On Friday, the builder may burn time trying to preserve the screen-only story when the safer submission path is pre-rendered receipt overlays sourced from `receipt.md` and `review.json`. Conversely, if overlays are used, the README/script should not imply every money shot was reached by live UI navigation.
findings.suggested_action: Make the operational rule explicit: screen-only CS UI for b4 and b6a; pre-captured artifact overlay allowed and preferred for b5 funnel/NAB2 and b6b reviewer flags. Label overlays as receipt artifacts from CS, not generic post-production claims.

findings.title: Raw CS artifacts still contain `validated` wording that can leak into screenshots
findings.evidence: The README says the Reviewer flagged `validated` and `definitive` and they were edited out, but repo search shows `docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/live-fullsweep-loose/*.json` and `stage5/chain.json` still contain phrases like `consistent with a validated gene -> program -> disease chain`. The safe reviewer evidence is `stage5/review.json`, not arbitrary raw artifact views.
findings.concern_path: If the Friday capture opens the wrong artifact or uses a broad static overlay, the video can accidentally show the exact calibrated-language violation it claims was removed.
findings.suggested_action: Whitelist screenshot sources: `stage5/review.json` for the reviewer flags, the curated `receipt_chain.md`/README for calibrated claims, and avoid raw `live-fullsweep-loose` JSON in the video unless cropped to fields that do not include `validated`.

escalated_findings.rationale: Accepted in debate but not addressed in repo: b5 artifact-open and b6b reviewer-proof remain TODOs in `cs/scenes.mjs` and unsolved mechanics in `CAPTURE_PLAN.md`.
Accepted conceptually, but the promised cached/live caption is absent from the runnable scene and narration files.
Accepted conceptually, but the artifact of record still says `a connection the literature had never drawn` rather than scoping to six co-mentions/no direct chain.
Accepted conceptually, but the video text still says `Its hardest confounder - refuted live` without naming the CRISPRi cis-artifact scope or GWAS-label caveat.
Accepted strongly, but no concrete NO receipt is wired into b5; the current scene only asserts a confident NO in a caption.
Accepted conceptually, but `cs/narration.mjs` and the endcap still use one-platform/product-maturity language rather than the grounded workbench wording.
Accepted conceptually, but b6b still depends on reaching hidden `review.json`/up-transcript evidence and has no prepared artifact overlay in the capture plan.

Partially unresolved: the env var spelling appears internally consistent as `ELLEVENLABS_API_KEY`, but status language remains stale because capture is still described as de-risked while blocker TODOs remain.

dropped_findings.rationale: Claude's primary-CS plan with an explicit hybrid fallback is a reasonable final-week tradeoff. The remaining risk is now captured under the release-blocker and overlay findings rather than as an argument to abandon the CS-native cut.

The Swanson segment is still long, but Claude's choice to keep it as the concept-carrier is defensible for Researcher-track framing. It is not a blocker if the recovered seconds from b5/b6 are used for visible receipts.

recommended_next_question: Final-round recommendation: do not debate the concept further; patch the actual video artifacts and pre-capture four required frames: live micro-sweep proof, cached/full-sweep disclosure plus NAB2 receipt, one concrete NO receipt, and reviewer `validated/definitive` flag.
